description: Session 10 golden set for retrieval evaluation. Five queries tuned to surface differences between vector-only (A), hybrid (B), rerank (C) and hybrid+rerank (D): Q1 is a short keyword where FTS should rescue a budget the narrow vector recall misses; Q2 is another keyword where wide recall plus cross-encoder should reorder; Q3/Q4 are cases where reranking regresses despite good vector rankings; Q5 is a long natural-language baseline where all configs tie. relevant_budget_ids assigned via AI-assisted annotation against data/budgets_sample.json (chunk_type=budget_component). A retrieved chunk counts as relevant iff its parent budget_id is in relevant_budget_ids.

queries.note: Hybrid win: FTS matches both e-commerce budgets; narrow vector recall (top_k=5) often surfaces only one Stripe chunk.
Rerank win: vector lumps IND-001/IND-003 industrial telemetry with IND-004; cross-encoder promotes the wind-farm SCADA budget.
Rerank regression: bi-encoder already ranks both healthcare budgets at the top; reranking promotes HLT-004 pharmacy noise.
Rerank regression: vector keeps ECO-001 checkout chunks high; reranker demotes them below adjacent e-commerce budgets.

queries.query: Telemedicine platform for patient appointment scheduling, secure messaging and remote monitoring of connected medical devices.
queries.note: Baseline: long NL query where all four configs reach the same precision — useful as a sanity check, not a differentiator.
